rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 5, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Bryan Steam LLC¿s motion for summary judgment alternate motion for summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 5, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages are denied. As to summary judgment, Mr. Shortall¿s deposition testimony and declaration and defendant¿s responses to standard interrogatories (Exhibit JJ) create a triable issue whether Mr. Murphy was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Shortall¿s declaration are overruled. As to summary adjudication, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ responses to contention interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA ANN NEAL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 5, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Todd Pacific Shipyards Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of showing a complete defense to plaintiff¿s cause of action. Defendant did not demonstrate that the California Workers¿ Compensation exclusivity doctrine bars all of plaintiff¿s claims against it. Plaintiff concedes that Workers¿ Compensation is the exclusive remedy for Mrs. Neal¿s direct occupational exposure to asbestos while working for defendant. However, plaintiff also alleges secondary (take-home) exposure of Mrs. Neal through Clifford Neal, also a Todd employee. Defendant did not demonstrate that all of Mrs. Neal¿s alleged exposures to asbestos attributable to defendant arose out of and in the course of her employment. (See, e.g., LaTourette v. Workers¿ Compensation Appeals Board (1998) 17 Cal.4th 644, 650-652.) Further, the derivative injury doctrine is inapplicable. Snyder v. Michael¿s Stores, Inc. (1997) 16 Cal.4th 991, cited by both parties, gives support for the conclusion that plaintiff¿s claims are not barred by Workers¿ Compensation, as plaintiff¿s claim is not legally dependent on any possible injury suffered by Clifford Neal. The personal comfort doctrine does not apply. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 5, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6, Defendant McMillan Brothers Electric, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories and Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Peterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony and declaration are overruled. The court only considered this as a motion for summary judgment. Although defendant¿s caption titles it a motion for summary judgment or in the alternative motion for summary adjudication, defendant¿s notice and separate statement failed to state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 5, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6, Defendant Cupertino Electric, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Peterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories and Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony and the caption, appearance and reporter pages related to it are overruled. The court only considered this as a motion for summary judgment. Although defendant¿s caption titles it a motion for summary judgment or in the alternative motion for summary adjudication, defendant¿s notice and separate statement failed to state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 5, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6, Defendant Robertshaw Controls Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (duty) and off calendar as to issues 2, 3 and 4 (strict liability, false representation and punitive damages). Dr. Cohen¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Peterson. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Cohen¿s declaration are overruled. Issues 2, 3 and 4 are off calendar because plaintiffs have dismissed their strict liability and false representation causes of action and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. rulings.Case Title: JEREMY SUCH, AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 5, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 7, Defendant Temporary Plant Cleanrs, Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Although not cited by defendant, Bockrath is the controlling authority. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
